throws if used outside DataTable"},{"name":"compareValues","type":"utility","generic":"T","params":["a","b","col","dir","extract"],"returns":"number","description":"Null-safe comparator supporting strings, numbers, and fallback string coercion"},{"name":"DataTableComponent","type":"React function component","generic":"TData, TColumn","params":["props: DataTableProps"],"returns":"JSX.Element","description":"Main table component rendering thead/tbody with sorting, selection, pagination"},{"name":"body","type":"inner render helper","description":"Renders error, loading, empty, or row states inside DataTableComponent"},{"name":"ariaSort","type":"inner helper","description":"Returns aria-sort attribute value based on current sort"},{"name":"sortMark","type":"inner helper","description":"Returns visual sort indicator (▲/▼)"},{"name":"handleSort","type":"useCallback","description":"Tri-state sort cycler: asc → desc → none"}],"classes":[],"interfaces_and_types":["SortDirection","SortState","ColumnDef","PaginationState","FetchResult","UseDataFetchOptions","SelectionCtx","DataTableProps"],"contexts":["SelectionContext"],"imports":[{"module":"react","named":["createContext","useCallback","useContext","useEffect","useMemo","useRef","useState"],"default":"React"}],"exports":{"default":"DataTable","named":["useDataFetch","useTableSelection","useDebounce","SortDirection","SortState","ColumnDef","PaginationState","FetchResult","UseDataFetchOptions","DataTableProps"]},"complexity_notes":"Moderate-to-high complexity. Combines several concerns: (1) a generic debounce hook, (2) a stateful data-fetch hook with AbortController-based cancellation and stale-response guarding via an `ok` flag, (3) React Context for selection state, (4) a generic comparator with null handling and type-specific comparison, and (5) a large presentational component with accessibility (ARIA roles, aria-sort, aria-selected, aria-busy, aria-current, keyboard support for sortable headers). Uses TypeScript generics heavily (TData, TColumn, TFilters). Pagination window logic computes a sliding 5-page range. Some code smells: use of comma-operator with short-circuit (`ok || (setData(...), setPagination(...))`) is terse and non-idiomatic; the `ok` cleanup pattern coexists with AbortController which is redundant. Sorting is done client-side on already-paginated data, which may produce inconsistent ordering across pages when server-side pagination is used. Overall well-structured, accessible, and reusable."}
